name: Tex-Mex Wontons
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the ground beef, chopped onion, and green pepper in a large skillet. Cook until the meat is browned, stirring to crumble it.
2. Drain any excess pan drippings from the skillet and discard them.
3. Add refried beans, shredded cheddar cheese, catsup, and chili powder to the skillet. Stir well to combine all ingredients.
4. Place a small spoonful of beef mixture in the center of each wonton skin.
5. Fold the top corner over the filling, then fold the side corners over as well.
6. Roll up the wontons in a jellyroll fashion (like a cinnamon roll) and moisten the edges with water to seal them.
7. Heat vegetable oil to 375 degrees Fahrenheit in a wok or large skillet.
8. Carefully place six wontons in the hot oil, frying for 30 seconds on each side until golden brown.
9. Remove the fried wontons from the oil using tongs and drain on paper towels.
10. Repeat with the remaining wontons.
11. Serve the Tex-Mex Wontons with Taco Sauce or Picante Sauce.
